checks.a_analysis: The output elements all correspond to content found in the source document. There is no evidence of fabricated body text, invented section headings, or other non-existent prose. AI-generated metadata like the title is an acceptable addition for accessibility.

All phone numbers and emails present in the output (e.g., 919-754-6672, sandra.rosser@nc.gov, 919-754-6000) exactly match the source. No URLs or contact details were hallucinated. Note that one URL is missing from the output, which is addressed in check C5.
All three pages of the source document are represented in the output. The header info, scope, instructions, vendor list, terms, and history are all present.
The tables (Contract box on page 1 and History on pages 2-3) accurately reflect the data rows found in the source. No significant data rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are two significant text omissions where the pipeline skipped linked text. 1) On page 1, the phrase 'ITS Service Catalog' is missing between elements 9 and 11 (reading order 10 is missing), resulting in the sentence reading '...through the in which you...'. 2) On page 2, the URL 'https://it.nc.gov/services/service-directory/telephony/cellular-telephone' is entirely omitted after element 18. These omissions are major as they remove the specific destinations (the catalog and the website) required to follow the contract's instructions.
checks.b_evidence_source: Customers will process all cellular requests through the ITS Service Catalog in which you... [AND] ...access Vendor’s website, CLICK HERE https://it.nc.gov/services/service-directory/telephony/cellular-telephone
checks.b_evidence_output: Customers will process all cellular requests through the [END OF ELEMENT 9] in which you can access... [END OF ELEMENT 11] [AND] To obtain contract information or access Vendor's website, [END OF ELEMENT 18]
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1, Instructions section; Page 2, first section.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Wording and dates (including the unusual '2023-24-24' date) have been preserved faithfully from the source document. No unauthorized modifications to legal language or factual data were found.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text or sections from the source document. Each paragraph, table row, and heading appears exactly once, matching the source PDF structure.

The heading hierarchy is preserved and logically ordered. H1 is used for the main title, H2 for major sections (Scope, Vendor Plans, Authorized Vendors, etc.), and H3 correctly nested for 'Instructions' under the 'Vendor Plans' section. There are no skips in hierarchy levels.

The table structures are faithfully converted. The contact information box on page 1 is linearized into logical rows. The History table, which spans pages 2 and 3, is split into two table objects in the JSON, which preserves the tabular relationship of dates to events without corruption of columns or rows.

The reading order fails significantly due to missing content within sentences. In the 'Instructions' section (elements 9 and 11), the flow is broken by the absence of element 10, leaving the sentence 'through the in which you can access' nonsensical. Similarly, on page 2, element 18 ends abruptly, and the subsequent information (the link and URL) is missing entirely before skipping to element 21. This materially disrupts the logical flow of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Customers will process all cellular requests through the ITS Service Catalog in which you can access... To obtain contract information or access Vendor’s website, CLICK HERE https://it.nc.gov/services/service-directory/telephony/cellular-telephone
checks.b_evidence_output: Customers will process all cellular requests through the [MISSING] in which you can access... To obtain contract information or access Vendor's website, [MISSING]

checks.a_analysis: The list of Authorized Vendors is converted into separate paragraph elements. While not structured as an explicit 'list' type, the meaning and visual sequence of the list items are preserved accurately.

Several critical links and URLs present in the source PDF are missing from the output. The 'ITS Service Catalog' link on page 1 and the 'CLICK HERE' link along with the specific government URL on page 2 are entirely absent from the JSON elements.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 1, Reading Order 9-11; Section 2, Reading Order 18

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-page document is converted into a single-column scrolling layout, which is appropriate for HTML. The introduction of a Table of Contents (TOC) at the top/side is an expected accessibility feature. The key information from the contract header box in the PDF is organized into a clear table format in the HTML. Section headings and paragraphs follow the same logical sequence as the original.

The contract information at the top of page 1 and the 'History' section starting on page 2 are correctly rendered as HTML tables. The tables have clear borders or background shading to distinguish rows. Although the 'History' table is split into two distinct tables in the HTML corresponding to the page breaks in the PDF, the data within them is readable, aligned, and logically structured. Header labels in the contract information table are correctly associated with their values.

The State Seal of North Carolina, which appears in the top header of the source PDF, is present in the HTML output. While its position has been shifted (appearing between the 'Instructions' and 'Authorized Vendors' sections), the image is rendered correctly and is not broken.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content in the HTML output. Paragraphs and table entries appear only once. The presence of section titles in both the Table of Contents and the body is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute a defect.
